items.quote: No engagement from Jason Savarese (Partner), Ken Litton (MD), or Cade Flitter (VP) in 6.5 months despite $500k deal size
items.source: OpportunityContactRole, Next Step history, Task history

items.quote: No quantified ROI, cost savings, TCO comparison vs Ariba, or business case documented
items.source: Next Step history, Salesforce Business_Objectives__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c

items.quote: Action in process to qualify if this is one-off or part of the suite replacement
No documented decision process, RFI/RFP timeline, or approval gates
No MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec timeline documented

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, cost savings, or ROI documented across 6.5 months. Next Step 26 Mar states Lauren 'Looking for an AI based Vendor Management System' confirming AI appetite and a qualitative vendor-management pain, but no financial impact quantified (TCO comparison vs Ariba, supplier onboarding cycle-time savings, AI-driven negotiation impact, headcount reduction, error-rate improvement). Without quantified metrics, Economic Buyer cannot secure budget approval for $500k investment, and deal remains stuck in Qualified. The qualitative pain exists but is insufficient for enterprise procurement to advance. Quantifying the value case is the immediate gap to close.

champion.narrative: Lauren Flores (Product Director, Source to Pay) is sole contact and showing champion-like traits: she registered for Pinnacle (Zycus event), accepted Houston Rodeo invite, engaged on personal email, and requested AI-based VMS demos. Next Step history from Jan-Jun 2026 shows Arthur maintaining regular personal and professional touch (lunch invites, event invites, congratulations on her role). However, Lauren's influence is unproven and at risk: the 17 Jun demo was cancelled due to 'Other priorities,' she could not attend Houston Rodeo or Pinnacle due to compliance/recording concerns, and she has not multi-threaded to Jason Savarese (Partner), Ken Litton (MD), or Cade Flitter (VP) despite their contact-role presence. Lauren's access to Economic Buyer is unknown, and no evidence of her driving internal advocacy, introducing senior stakeholders, or pushing back on competitors. Champion strength is developing but fragile: if she loses influence or changes roles, Zycus has no backup relationship. Securing her active advocacy and multi-threading through her is the immediate priority.

competition.narrative: Four competitors confirmed across Salesforce and Next Step: SAP Ariba (primary, incumbent per Next Step 17 Jun 'checking for timing for Ariba replacement'), Coupa, Spend HQ, and Sievo (Salesforce Competitors__c). Ariba is the key battleground: this is a replacement deal, and Goldman's existing Ariba investment, integration depth, and inertia are significant switching barriers. No documented competitive review, no recent differentiation conversation, and no evidence Zycus has positioned Merlin/Agentic AI as the wedge against Ariba's static workflow. Next Step 26 Mar shows Lauren wants 'AI based Vendor Management System,' which is the opening for Merlin negotiation agent and I2O, but no follow-up on whether that narrative landed. How we win: position Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA (all in Products scope) as the AI-native differentiation Ariba cannot match, center the demo on supplier onboarding automation and AI-driven negotiation ROI, and tie it to a quantified TCO comparison favoring Zycus. Without this wedge activation, Ariba inertia will win.

identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational pain articulated by the relevant stakeholder: 'Looking for an AI based Vendor Management System' (Next Step 26 Mar). This confirms a qualitative pain around vendor/supplier management and a desire for AI capability, and implicitly dissatisfaction with the current Ariba stack (since Products scope includes Merlin, iSupplier, and Agentic AI). Next Step 4 Mar notes 'Action in process to qualify if this is one-off or part of the suite replacement,' indicating the pain may extend beyond VMS to full S2P replacement, but scope remains unclear. No quantified pain (cost, cycle time, error rate, compliance risk) documented. Pain owner is Lauren Flores (Product Director), but without Economic Buyer or senior stakeholder confirmation, pain lacks enterprise-level urgency. The 17 Jun demo cancellation suggests competing internal priorities may be diluting this pain's urgency.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process unknown. No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) documented. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c visible as a forcing function. No SI/partner role identified despite the scale and complexity of an S2P replacement at Goldman Sachs, where systems integration, ERP touchpoints, and InfoSec review are standard gates. Next Step references a personal connection to 'her husband at BMS Cat' and intro to Bailey Erazo, hinting at a possible partner or services angle, but no formalized partner scoping. Without visibility into contracting and InfoSec timelines, close-date defensibility is unknown. Uncovering the paper process and any contract expiration or budget-approval deadline is essential to validate the 29 Jan 2027 close date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not engaged after 6.5 months despite $500k deal size and 7-month close horizon. Jason Savarese (Partner, Global Division Head Corporate Planning and Management) and Ken Litton (Managing Director, Corporate Planning & Management) are contact roles and carry budget authority by title, but neither appears in Next Step history, tasks, or any documented buyer interaction. Lauren Flores (Product Director, Source to Pay) is sole contact but lacks authority to approve $500k budget or drive enterprise S2P replacement alone. No pricing or commercial conversation documented. This is a critical gap: without EB activation, deal cannot progress past Qualified. The absence of EB engagement for 6.5 months in a $500k enterprise deal signals either weak internal sponsorship or insufficient urgency.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process unknown. No call sequence or stakeholder progression visible (no Avoma calls discovered for this deal). Contact roles include Partner, MD, VP, and Product Director, suggesting a multi-level approval chain, but no documented process map, no approval gates, no timeline for RFI/RFP/shortlisting, and no confirmed procurement channel. Salesforce Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c not referenced in available evidence. The 17 Jun demo cancellation due to 'Other priorities' and the 3+ month scope ambiguity ('one-off or part of suite replacement') indicate Goldman has not committed to a structured evaluation process. Without a defined decision process, deal cannot advance predictably. Mapping the process and approval chain is the critical next step.

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial signal from Next Step entries: 26 Mar planned Zoom call on 'Supplier / Vendor Master (lets get an internal read for MerlinSupplier Onboarding!)' suggests supplier onboarding is a decision criterion, and 19 May demo of 'iSupplier, Portal and iRisk as first module' indicates portal access and risk management are in scope. 26 Mar 'Looking for an AI based Vendor Management System' confirms AI capability is a criterion. However, no structured RFI/RFP, no documented evaluation scorecard, no use-case validation sessions, and no buyer-articulated decision criteria beyond these scattered Next Step clues. Scope ambiguity ('one-off or part of the suite replacement' per 4 Mar Next Step) means decision criteria remain unclear: if this is a narrow VMS buy, full S2P suite criteria are irrelevant; if suite replacement, criteria are under-defined. The 17 Jun demo cancellation and lack of follow-up suggest Goldman has not formalized their evaluation process.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are null, so no AI excitement score from Salesforce. However, Next Step 26 Mar provides direct buyer signal: Lauren Flores stated 'Looking for an AI based Vendor Management System,' confirming AI appetite and curiosity. This is a buyer-initiated AI interest, not rep-led, which qualifies as AI Curious tier. No evidence of AI resistance, but also no evidence of quantified AI-ROI analysis or AI-driven evaluation criteria, so not yet AI Hungry. Tier is AI Curious based on buyer's stated interest without quantified urgency.

Next Step history shows regular rep-initiated engagement (personal outreach, event invites, demo scheduling) from Jan-Jun 2026, but buyer momentum is weak: Lauren Flores could not attend Pinnacle or Houston Rodeo, 19 May demo was set, 17 Jun demo was cancelled due to 'Other priorities,' and 29 Jun rep reached out to follow up on lunch and check timing for Ariba replacement. LastActivityDate 26 Jun and rep outreach 29 Jun show deal is LIVE but stalled in qualification with no structured evaluation process launched.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer engagement in 6.5 months despite $500k deal size and 7-month horizon. Jason Savarese (Partner), Ken Litton (MD), and Cade Flitter (VP) are contact roles but not activated. Lauren Flores (Product Director) is sole contact and lacks authority to approve budget or drive enterprise S2P replacement alone.

17 Jun demo cancelled due to 'Other priorities' per Next Step, signaling competing internal initiatives are deprioritizing the Ariba replacement. Buyer's internal urgency is unclear, and no compelling event (contract expiration, budget approval deadline) is documented to drive near-term action.

No quantified ROI, cost savings, or business case documented in 6.5 months. Without a financial justification (TCO comparison vs Ariba, supplier onboarding cycle-time savings, AI-driven negotiation impact), Economic Buyer cannot secure budget approval for $500k investment.

Scope ambiguity unresolved for 3+ months: 'Action in process to qualify if this is one-off or part of the suite replacement' per Next Step (4 Mar). Deal size, Products scope, and stakeholder alignment depend on scope clarity. If this is a narrow one-off module buy, $500k amount and full S2P suite positioning may be misaligned.

Single-thread risk: Lauren Flores is sole contact after 6.5 months with no multi-threading to senior stakeholders, procurement leadership, or IT/InfoSec. If Lauren loses internal influence, changes roles, or the initiative gets reassigned, Zycus has no backup relationship. The 17 Jun cancellation suggests her influence may already be weakening.

Competing against SAP Ariba (incumbent), Coupa, Spend HQ, and Sievo. Ariba replacement creates switching cost and integration risk. No competitive review in 6.5 months, no Zycus differentiation anchored, and no AI-ROI wedge activated despite Lauren's AI interest. Without clear differentiation, Ariba inertia will prevail.

No evidence of her driving internal advocacy, introducing senior stakeholders, or pushing back on competitors. Champion strength is developing but fragile.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is LIVE per engagement pulse: LastActivityDate 26 Jun (4 days ago) and rep outreach 29 Jun show active engagement. However, buyer momentum is weak: 17 Jun demo cancelled due to 'Other priorities,' Lauren Flores is sole contact with no multi-threading, and no Economic Buyer or senior stakeholder engagement in 6.5 months. Sentiment is neutral: no buyer enthusiasm documented, no quantified ROI, and no structured evaluation process launched. Deal is not cooling or dark but is stalled in Qualified with unclear buyer urgency.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal age 202 days (created 10 Dec 2025), 213 days to close (29 Jan 2027). Time in Qualified stage: 202 days with no submission-date progression. No Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, no Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c recorded. To close 29 Jan 2027 from Qualified requires advancing through Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation/Validation, and Contract stages in 213 days. Typical enterprise S2P cycle is 12-15 months; this deal has consumed 6.5+ months in discovery/qualification with no visible progression to structured evaluation (RFI/RFP). Required pace: reach Vendor Selected by Oct 2026 (4 months) to allow 3 months for negotiation and contracting. Current pace will not meet close date. No forward slip visible in field history but LastActivityDate 26 Jun and rep outreach 29 Jun show engagement is active, not stalled.
evidence: Stage Qualified, 202 days with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted per submission dates

flags: Run a joint ROI workshop with Lauren and Finance/Procurement leadership to quantify the Ariba replacement business case. No cost savings, cycle time reduction, or headcount impact is documented. A quantified ROI model (TCO comparison vs Ariba, supplier onboarding cycle-time savings, AI-driven negotiation impact) is the forcing function to secure EB commitment and create urgency. Position Merlin capabilities as the financial lever.

Resolve the scope ambiguity (one-off vendor management module vs full S2P suite replacement) to align deal size, Products scope, and internal stakeholders. This question has been open since 4 Mar per Next Step and is blocking a formal evaluation process. Use the week-of-29-Jun call to get Lauren's definitive answer, then right-size the opportunity amount and messaging accordingly.

best_practice_check.summary: Single-thread risk through Lauren Flores (Product Director) with no Economic Buyer activation after 6.5 months in a $500k deal. No quantified ROI or business case. Scope ambiguity unresolved for 3+ months. 17 Jun demo cancellation signals deprioritization. Deal is LIVE (rep outreach 29 Jun, LastActivityDate 26 Jun) but momentum is weak and multi-threading is absent.

competitive_position.summary: Competing primarily against SAP Ariba (incumbent, replacement deal per Next Step 17 Jun), plus Coupa, Spend HQ, and Sievo. Ariba is the primary threat due to incumbent advantage, integration depth, and switching inertia. Lauren Flores expressed interest in 'AI based Vendor Management System' (26 Mar), creating an opening for Merlin/Agentic AI differentiation, but no evidence this narrative has been activated or that Zycus has positioned AI-native capabilities as the wedge. No competitive review documented in past 6.5 months. Sentiment is neutral: no buyer preference stated, no competitive displacement signals, and no Zycus differentiation anchored in buyer's mind. Risk: without a clear AI-ROI wedge and quantified TCO comparison, Ariba inertia will prevail.

Multi-thread to Economic Buyer tier (Jason Savarese Partner, Ken Litton MD, Cade Flitter VP) to secure budget authority and executive sponsorship. Lauren Flores (Product Director) is sole contact after 6.5 months; without EB engagement this deal cannot advance beyond Qualified or secure $500k approval. Leverage Arthur's relationship with Lauren to request an introduction to Savarese or Litton, framed as validating strategic fit for the Ariba replacement initiative.

Run a joint ROI workshop with Lauren and Finance/Procurement leadership to quantify the Ariba replacement business case. No cost savings, cycle time reduction, or headcount impact is documented. A quantified ROI model (TCO comparison vs Ariba, supplier onboarding cycle-time savings, AI-driven negotiation impact) is the forcing function to secure EB commitment and create urgency. Position Merlin capabilities as the financial lever.

Resolve the scope ambiguity (one-off vendor management module vs full S2P suite replacement) to align deal size, Products scope, and internal stakeholders. This question has been open since 4 Mar per Next Step and is blocking a formal evaluation process. Use the week-of-29-Jun call to get Lauren's definitive answer, then right-size the opportunity amount and messaging accordingly.

gaps: No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity via opp_id or account-level discovery; unable to reconstruct buyer-voice MEDDPICC signals from transcripts. MEDDPICC read is built from Salesforce Next Step, contact roles, Products, and competitor fields only.

No submission dates (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c) populated, so true time-in-stage and stage velocity cannot be computed from submission-date series. Time-in-stage derived from CreatedDate only.

No field history for Amount, CloseDate, or Stage visible in tools available this sweep, so forward slip and stage progression cannot be reconstructed. Movement summary built from Next Step history and LastActivityDate only.

AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null; AI excitement tier inferred from Next Step buyer quote ('Looking for an AI based Vendor Management System') only, not from scored AIS signal.
No Tasks or Events detail read this sweep (tools available returned no task/event data); open_deliverables and contact recency built from Next Step history only.
